result: If I could change the world in one way, it would be to instill a universal sense of empathy in every person. Empathy—the ability to truly understand and share the feelings of others—could break down barriers of misunderstanding, reduce conflict, and foster cooperation on a global scale. It would mean people genuinely considering the perspectives and struggles of others, whether they’re across the street or across the world. I believe this could lead to more equitable systems, less violence, and a deeper collective commitment to solving problems like poverty, climate change, and injustice. It wouldn’t erase differences, but it might make us better at navigating them. That’s the change I’d choose.
